evals.name: planted-decisions-synthetic-repo
evals.prompt: This repo has no ADRs — go through the git history and backfill decision records for the architecturally significant decisions.
evals.expected_output: A docs/decisions/ directory containing DRs for the planted decisions (RabbitMQ adoption, SQLite-to-PostgreSQL migration, Docker+CI introduction, RabbitMQ-to-SQS replacement). The RabbitMQ/SQS pair recorded as two cross-linked DRs (superseded + adopted). Each DR dated with the commit date, listing evidence commits and a provenance line. No DRs for noise commits.
assertions: Decision records are written to docs/decisions/ (the log-decision default for a repo with no existing DR directory)
All four planted decisions are covered: RabbitMQ adoption, SQLite-to-PostgreSQL migration, Docker+CI introduction, RabbitMQ-to-SQS replacement
The RabbitMQ-to-SQS reversal is recorded as two cross-linked records, with the original RabbitMQ record marked superseded
Each record's date metadata is the historical commit date (2022-2024), not today's date
Every record contains a provenance line stating it was reconstructed retroactively from git history
No records are written for noise commits (lodash bump, lint fixes, readme typo, retry-logic fix)
Every record cites at least one real commit hash that exists in the repo's history

evals.name: madr-dedup-and-naming
evals.prompt: Reconstruct decision records from this repo's git history for any significant decisions we never documented.
evals.expected_output: The already-documented PostgreSQL decision is skipped (dedup against adr/0002-use-postgresql.md). The undocumented Docker adoption gets a new record matching the existing MADR naming convention (adr/0003-*.md), dated 2022-03-22 with a provenance line.
assertions: No new record duplicates the PostgreSQL decision already documented in adr/0002-use-postgresql.md
The undocumented Docker adoption gets a new decision record
The new record matches the existing MADR naming convention and numbering (adr/0003-*.md), not a forked DR-0001-*.md scheme
The new record's date is the Docker commit date (2022-03-22), not today's date
The new record contains a provenance line stating it was reconstructed retroactively from git history
The new record cites the real Docker-introduction commit hash

evals.name: real-repo-agent-skills
evals.prompt: we never wrote down why this repo is set up the way it is - can you reconstruct the decision records from git history?
evals.expected_output: Finds the genuine 'replace git submodule with auto-clone sibling pattern' decision (commit 6c96217) and writes a well-evidenced DR with commit references and a provenance line, without fabricating decisions from noise commits.
files: clone of the agent-skills repo itself
assertions: A record documents the replacement of git submodules with the auto-clone sibling pattern for external skills (commit 6c96217)
Every record cites at least one real commit hash from the repo's history
Every record contains a provenance line stating it was reconstructed retroactively from git history
Each record's date metadata is the historical commit date, not today's date
No record describes a decision that lacks supporting commit evidence (no fabrication)
